title: Lab 36: Persistent NFS Direct Mount
description: Mount a remote NFS export persistently using /etc/fstab

tasks: On server, create /exports/direct36, place nfs36.txt in it, export it read-only to 192.168.122.0/24, and enable nfs-server.
On client, mount 192.168.122.3:/exports/direct36 persistently at /mnt/direct36 with ro,sync options and verify nfs36.txt is readable.
hints: Do not use autofs for this task.
checks: # server test -f /exports/direct36/nfs36.txt && grep -Eq '^/exports/direct36[[:space:]]+192\.168\.122\.0/24' /etc/exports.d/lab36.exports && systemctl is-active nfs-server | grep -qx active
solution_outline: Configure a direct persistent NFS mount.
solution_commands: grep -Fqx '192.168.122.3:/exports/direct36 /mnt/direct36 nfs ro,sync 0 0' /etc/fstab || echo '192.168.122.3:/exports/direct36 /mnt/direct36 nfs ro,sync 0 0' >> /etc/fstab
for attempt in 1 2 3 4 5; do mountpoint -q /mnt/direct36 && test -f /mnt/direct36/nfs36.txt && break; sleep 2; done
